records.ety: [F. sardonique, L. sardonius, Gr. <?/, <?/, perhaps fr. <?/ to grin like a dog, or from a certain plant of Sardinia, Gr. <?/, which was said to screw up the face of the eater.]

defs: Forced; unnatural; insincere; hence, derisive, mocking, malignant, or bitterly sarcastic; -- applied only to a laugh, smile, or some facial semblance of gayety.
quotes: Where strained, sardonic smiles are glozing still, And grief is forced to laugh against her will.
The scornful, ferocious, sardonic grin of a bloody ruffian.

defs: Of, pertaining to, or resembling, a kind of linen made at Colchis.
